data.title: 36Kr Holdings Inc. releases unaudited financial results for H1 2025, serving New Economy participants in China.
data.description: In Beijing on September 26, 2025, 36Kr Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ: KRKR) disclosed its unaudited financial results for the first half of 2025. Known for its focus on supporting New Economy players in China, 36Kr is a well-established entity in the industry.

data.title: Li Auto Inc. launches Li i6, a 5-seat battery electric SUV, priced at RMB249,800, with delivery from Sept. 27, 2025.
data.description: In Beijing, China on September 26, 2025, Li Auto Inc. unveiled the Li i6, a cutting-edge five-seater battery electric SUV, solidifying its position in China's new energy vehicle sector. Priced at RMB249,800 for the standard setup, the Li i6 is set to be available for delivery starting September 27, 2025. Those interested in learning more about this innovative vehicle can find additional information on Li Auto’s official website.

data.title: Nvidia investment in OpenAI sparks concerns of circular loop with startup buying Nvidia chips, impacting stock. Investors worried about the rationale.
data.description: Nvidia Corporation's recent investment in OpenAI has caused concern among investors regarding a possible circular investment loop. This situation involves Nvidia investing in startups that may then purchase Nvidia chips or cloud services built on Nvidia GPUs. Analyst Stacy Rasgon from Bernstein acknowledged these concerns and raised questions about the rationale behind the move and its potential impact on Nvidia's stock. Despite the worries, Nvidia clarified that the investment funds from the OpenAI deal would not be used to buy its own products. OpenAI, led by Sam Altman, recently made a significant cloud deal with Oracle, potentially increasing Oracle's Nvidia chip purchases. Bank of America analysts, led by Vivek Arya, project positive outcomes for Nvidia's future. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Progress Software, NASDAQ: PRGS, to release Q3 earnings Sept 29. Forecast: $1.30 EPS, $240.11M revenue - up from $1.26 EPS, $178.69M revenue last year. Stock price: $41.20.
data.description: Progress Software Corporation (NASDAQ: PRGS) is set to announce its third-quarter earnings on Monday, Sept. 29 after the market closes. Analysts are predicting earnings of $1.30 per share, an increase from $1.26 per share in the same period last year. The company, headquartered in Burlington, Massachusetts, is expected to report quarterly revenue of $240.11 million, up from $178.69 million a year ago. Progress Software exceeded earnings expectations in the second quarter. The company's shares closed at $41.20, rising by 0.2% on Thursday. For more information and to access the latest analyst ratings, readers can visit the Analyst Stock Ratings page on Benzinga.
